meta.purpose: DS component gotchas — usage traps in @npsin-oreo/design-system that are silent (they pass typecheck + every static gate) yet produce a real defect at render. Feeds gate 4 (lint_component_contracts.py, track D). Add an entry when a dogfood surfaces a new trap; the machine-checkable ones become lint rules.

notes.trap: className is spread onto the WRAPPER (data-slot=native-select-wrapper); the inner <select data-slot=native-select> is hardcoded h-8 (+ data-[size=sm]:h-7). So a height utility on <NativeSelect className=...> silently no-ops — the select renders 32px even next to a 48px input.
notes.fix: Set control height globally via the [data-slot=native-select] rule in globals.css (the scaffold's C2 control-parity block ships one), not via className on the element.
notes.surfaced_by: PARICH WMS draft-1 (32px select beside a 48px input passed all static gates)

notes.trap: A disabled control inside a modal trigger can't receive a click, so the dialog never opens — the primary action becomes unreachable in a walkthrough while looking present.
notes.fix: Keep the trigger enabled and guard the destructive/committing ACTION inside the dialog (or conditionally render the trigger), so the confirm step stays reachable.

notes.trap: Because className targets the wrapper, ANY inner-select style (text-size, padding, border) passed via className misses the <select>. Only wrapper-level layout (margin, width) lands.
notes.fix: Style the inner select through the [data-slot=native-select] selector in globals.css.
